mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate amplifies household purchasing power and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and deteriorating corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during recession onset periods, or produces positive returns in more than 50% of contraction months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue streams, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicate synchronized labor market and construction sector recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 55% of cases, or shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 600 thousand units signal robust residential investment and upstream manufacturing demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 600 thousand units seasonally adjusted
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or fails to predict returns when mortgage rates exceed 5.0%.
